channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: How Jimmy Carter's marriage to Rosalynn Carter helped shape his legacy
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter’s marriage to first lady Rosalynn Carter played a defining role in his life, spanning 77 years and making it the longest presidential marriage in U.S. history. Jimmy Carter died on Sunday, according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter 'was most impactful one-term president': Stuart Eizenstat
item.description: He also credited Carter for initiating defense programs that would later be associated with the Reagan administration.
item.dc:creator: Blake Burman, NewsNation

item.title: Five defining moments of Jimmy Carter's presidency
item.description: Former President Carter's post-presidential life lasted decades, putting his career in the White House in a deep rearview mirror in many ways. Carter died Sunday, according to the Carter Center.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Neukam, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Living US presidents react to death of former President Jimmy Carter
item.description: All five living U.S. presidents reacted to the death of former President Jimmy Carter, sharing memories as well as condolences to Carter's family.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia native, former President Jimmy Carter's influence stretched into Kern County
item.description: Jimmy Carter's single term as president and his remarkable and lengthy post-presidency left a mark on the nation in ways many may not appreciate today. But some here in Bakersfield most certainly did grasp his impact.
item.dc:creator: Robert Price, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politicians mourn Jimmy Carter's death
item.description: “Today, America and the world lost an extraordinary leader, statesman, and humanitarian,” President Joe Biden said.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: In Photos: Jimmy Carter
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter died on Sunday, according to the Carter Center. Here's a look at his life and times in photos. He had turned 100 in October, making him the longest-lived president in the nation’s history.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Jimmy Carter’s presidency: A time of 'malaise' that led to the election of Ronald Reagan
item.description: Numerous setbacks, both domestic and international, contributed to President Jimmy Carter's 1980 defeat at the hands of GOP challenger Ronald Reagan, making Carter a one-term president
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jimmy Carter, 39th president of the United States, dead at 100
item.description: Jimmy Carter, the 39th president of the United States who started the Department of Education and the Department of Energy, has died at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: European officials pitch new idea to shore up defenses with Trump's return
item.description: European officials are struggling to agree on a plan to shore up hundreds of billions of dollars to bolster defenses. Some NATO experts are proposing a global defense bank.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why Trump is targeting Panama, Greenland, Canada
item.description: President-elect Trump has spent parts of the holiday season vowing to take over the Panama Canal, calling for the U.S. to buy Greenland from Denmark and suggesting Canada could become the 51st state. It all suggests Trump is focused on somehow expanding the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: More battles over transgender rights await Supreme Court
item.description: Cases implicating transgender rights are piling up at the Supreme Court as it begins drafting its opinion on whether states can ban gender-affirming care for minors.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Trump appears to side with Musk, tech allies in debate over foreign workers roiling his supporters
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump appears to be siding with Elon Musk and his other backers in the tech industry as a dispute over immigration visas has divided his supporters.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge signals that contempt hearing for Rudy Giuliani over his assets might not go well for him
item.description: A federal judge is signaling that Rudy Giuliani's contempt hearing next Friday might not end so well for the former New York City mayor and onetime personal lawyer for President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Janet Yellen tells Congress US could hit debt limit in mid-January
item.description: "Treasury expects to hit the statutory debt ceiling between January 14 and January 23," Yellen wrote in a letter addressed to House and Senate leadership, at which point extraordinary measures would be used to prevent the government from breaching the nation's debt ceiling — which has been suspended
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US to send $1.25 billion in weapons to Ukraine, pushing to get aid out before Biden leaves office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States is expected to announce that it will send $1.25 billion in military assistance to Ukraine, U.S. officials said Friday, as the Biden administration pushes to get as much aid to Kyiv as possible before leaving office on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Baja to lease warehouse space to house deported migrants
item.description: He also stated the warehouse location will have easier access from the ports of entry where migrants might be deported instead of having to bus them to several areas in the city.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Trump has pressed for voting changes. GOP majorities in Congress will try to make that happen
item.description: Republicans plan to move quickly in their effort to overhaul the nation’s voting procedures, seeing an opportunity with control of the White House and both chambers of Congress to push through long-sought changes that include voter ID and proof-of-citizenship requirements.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump announces ambassador to Panama amid fight over canal
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced Miami-Dade County Commissioner Kevin Marino Cabrera as his pick for U.S. ambassador to Panama. The announcement comes as Trump is sparking a feud with the U.S. ally over control of the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump faces roadblocks to ambitious border crackdown
item.description: President-elect Trump has made ambitious promises on the border and immigration, including the biggest deportation operation ever seen. But much like in Trump’s first term in office, his immigration agenda faces significant roadblocks.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Former lawmaker Mary Murphy, longest-serving woman in the House, suffers stroke
item.description: Former Rep. Mary Murphy experienced complications after a stroke last week and will likely be transferred to hospice care, House Speaker Melissa Hortman said.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Bill Clinton is hospitalized with a fever but in good spirits, spokesperson says
item.description: The 78-year-old former president was hospitalized “for testing and observation” after developing a fever, his deputy chief of staff said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Ethics releases Matt Gaetz report: 5 takeaways
item.description: The report — which spans 42 pages and includes several more in exhibits — found “substantial evidence” that Gaetz violated House rules, state and federal laws, outlining allegations of prostitution, statutory rape and illicit drug use, among other accusations.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Biden didn't remove these 3 inmates from death row
item.description: Those who received the Monday commutations of their sentences saw them go from execution to life with no possibility of parole. However, three “hard cases” were not among the commutations.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Inside the Gaetz ethics report, a trove of new details alleging payments for sex and drug use
item.description: The 37-page report by the bipartisan panel includes explicit details of sex-filled parties and vacations that Matt Gaetz took part in while representing Florida's western panhandle. The findings conclude that he violated multiple state laws related to sexual misconduct while in office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden gives life in prison to 37 of 40 federal death row inmates
item.description: The move spares the lives of people convicted in killings, including the slayings of police and military officers, people on federal land and those involved in deadly bank robberies or drug deals, as well as the killings of guards or prisoners in federal facilities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Gaetz sues Ethics Committee in last-minute bid to block release of report
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) filed a lawsuit Monday against the House Ethics Committee in a last-minute attempt to block the panel from releasing its report on him.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC
